mnemonics.title: Types of Nested Classes
mnemonics.content: S I L A - Static, Inner (non-static), Local, Anonymous - remember as 'SIL A' sounds like 'I'll answer' for those who ask about types!

mnemonics.title: Static vs Non-Static Access
mnemonics.content: STATIC = NO instance needed, NO instance access. NON-STATIC = Instance needed, FULL access. Think: S-N-O-I-N (Static = No Instance = No access)

mnemonics.title: Local Inner Class Rule
mnemonics.content: Local variables must be FINAL or effectively final - think 'FL' (Final Local) for Local Inner classes

acronyms.expansion: Non-static Inner class Lacks explicit static - Inner classes are always associated with outer instance (Every Instance Requires Outer)
acronyms.usage: Remember that non-static inner classes always need an outer class instance to exist

acronyms.expansion: Static Anonymous Nested Dark (classes) - Static nested, Anonymous, Nested, (Member) Inner
acronyms.usage: All nested class types that can be declared with access modifiers

visualTips.concept: Static vs Non-Static Nested Class Relationship
visualTips.visualization: Draw a box for OuterClass. Inside, draw a smaller box for StaticNestedClass connected with a dotted line (no instance needed). For InnerClass, draw it connected with a solid line to an OuterClass instance shown outside the box.
Think of it as a 'temporary class' - show it as a cloud that extends/implements on-the-fly, with no separate named box for the class itself.
Draw a method frame (rectangle) with the local inner class inside it. Show a dotted line going out with an X mark indicating it cannot exit the method boundary.

analogies.analogy: Think of OuterClass as a house and InnerClass as a room inside the house. Just like a room needs the house to exist, an inner class needs an outer class instance. But a guest house (static nested) can exist independently.
Like hiring a temporary worker for one specific task without creating a full employee file - the worker (anonymous class) is created on the spot for that particular job.

Like a meeting room that only exists within the building - it cannot be accessed from outside the building (method scope), and people inside can only use the building's permanent facilities (final variables).
